---
title: "Why doesn't the refresh rate go any higher than 85Hz?"
date: 2007-05-15
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-15
My monitor = [Viewsonic P227F CRT 21"](http://www.dealtime.co.uk/xPF-ViewSonic-ViewSonic-P227fB-Display-CRT-21-2048-x-1536-79-Hz-0-2-mm-VGA-HD-15)
My Grahpics card = ATI Radeon 9600Pro

Here's the output of my xorg.conf file:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"ee"
	Option		"XkbVariant"	"ee"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AP [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	VideoRam	131072
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"P227f"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-130
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AP [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"P227f"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x960" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection
```


I have the graphics card drivers installed and I can use Beryl with all the nice eye-candy effects perfectly well with no problems whatsoever. But why doesn't the refresh rate go any HIGHER than 85Hz? When I try to select anything higher than 85Hz, it seems to apply it, but when I press a button on my monitor's front panel (which opens up an on-screen menu with all the details the monitor is currently using) it still shows 85Hz! And it's really staring to hurt my eyes. :(

For example, I'm currently using 1024x768 @ 85Hz. But in Windows I can use the same resolution at 150Hz.

The ideal resolution I would like to get is 1280x960 @ 120Hz, which is a proper 4:3 resolution, and works perfectly in Windows. As you can see I have added it to my xorg.conf, but it doesn't even show up in the selection box when I try to change my resotluion. Why?

---

### Post by mcduck on 2007-05-15
Check that the HorizSync & VertRefresh values in xorg.conf are correct for your monitor.

---

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-15
> **mcduck said:**
> Check that the HorizSync & VertRefresh values in xorg.conf are correct for your monitor.

Triple-checked it to make sure it's corrent. Tried restaring X, restarting the PC, and also swapping
between resolutions, but it still won't go any higher than 85Hz. :(  Thanks for replying, though.

---

### Post by mcduck on 2007-05-15
You could try adding a modeline to the Monitor section in xorg.conf for the resolution & refresh you want.

Here's one modeline generator: [http://xtiming.sourceforge.net/cgi-bin/xtiming.pl]("http://xtiming.sourceforge.net/cgi-bin/xtiming.pl")

---

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-16
Here's the modeline I managed to generate:
**Modeline "1280x960@120" 301.41 1280 1312 2456 2488 960 975 994 1009**

The modeline generator page said: *"Put the following modeline in your XF86Config in the Monitors section, and add it to your active modes in the Screen section. Use this modeline at your own risk."*

Ok, so I'm going to use it at my own risk (hopefully it will not break recently bought monitor). I tried inserting it to the monitor's section and the active modes section (at least I think I got it right, but what can you expect from a linux newbie?):

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-05-16
Do not insert a modeline in your screen section.  You only need it in your monitor section.  If that modeline does not work try:

> # 1280x960 @ 120.00 Hz (GTF) hsync: 123.48 kHz; pclk: 217.32 MHz
  
Modeline "1280x960_120.00"  217.32  1280 1376 1520 1760  960 961 964 1029  -HSync +Vsync

---

### Post by Wim Sturkenboom on 2007-05-16
See if it helps if you add the following line to the **device** section in the xorg.conf:
```

Option "UseEdidFreqs" "False"

```
Please give feedback; I know that it works for my nVidia card, but like to know if it also works for ATI.

---

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-16
[QUOTE=Wim Sturkenboom]See if it helps if you add the following line to the **device** section in the xorg.conf:
```

Option "UseEdidFreqs" "False"

```
Please give feedback; I know that it works for my nVidia card, but like to know if it also works for ATI.[/QUOTE]

YEAH, now we're getting somewhere, THANKS!  It almost works now. When I restart X, the screen goes all black and an on-screen message appears on my monitor, saying that it's "out of frequency", BUT when I typed in my username+password and logged in (it's using 1024x768 as a default resolution), I can now see that the 1280x960 @ 120Hz is is available from the Screen Resolution selection box!! :D

If I select it, however, the screen goes all black and an on-screen message appears on my monitor, saying that it's "out of frequency".
Then after 20 seconds of waiting, it reverts back to 1024x768.

I get the feeling all I need to do now, is to edit this modline just a little bit:
```
# 1280x960 @ 120.00 Hz (GTF) hsync: 123.48 kHz; pclk: 217.32 MHz
Modeline "1280x960_120.00" 217.32 1280 1376 1520 1760 960 961 964 1029 -HSync +Vsync
```

**alienexplorers**, what tool did you use to create this modeline?

### Post by Wim Sturkenboom on 2007-05-16

By the way, Why such a high refresh rate?

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-16
[QUOTE=Wim Sturkenboom]Take the modeline out and just add *1280x960* in the relevant subsection of screen; should work.

By the way, Why such a high refresh rate?[/QUOTE]

Already tried taking out the modeline, but if I do that, then the 1280x960 resolution will not even show up in the resolution selection box. And as you can see from my xorg.conf, I have already added this resolution next to the others.

Why such a high refresh rate? Well, my monitor supports up to 120Hz for 1280x1960 and up to 150Hz for 1024x768. The higher the refresh rate, the easier it is on the eyes. :)

### Post by Wim Sturkenboom on 2007-05-17
> **tanelt said:**
> Already tried taking out the modeline, but if I do that, then the 1280x960 resolution will not even show up in the resolution selection box. And as you can see from my xorg.conf, I have already added this resolution next to the others.I'm absolutely not sure about this one, but try *1280x960_120.00* instead of *1280x960* on the modes lines in the display subsections.

> **tanelt said:**
> Why such a high refresh rate? Well, my monitor supports up to 120Hz for 1280x1960 and up to 150Hz for 1024x768. The higher the refresh rate, the easier it is on the eyes. :)Although that's basically true, I wonder if you can see/feel/experience the difference; I personally don't (happy at 85 Hz).

In my opinion, pushing the elctronics in your monitor to its limits shortens the lifespan of the monitor. Therefor I only push it if I have to (this is when I use max resolution during graphics work where I like to keep the overview).

---

### Post by powder on 2007-05-22
Hi tanelt,

Sorry for my late reply.  I already know why you can't get 1280x960 working.  It's true, you need a custom modeline to achieve this resolution, but there is currently a regression in the fglrx driver that causes custom modelines to not work.  If you revert fglrx to ver 8.29.6 or use the open source ATI driver you should be able to achieve 1280x960.

P.S. Try this modeline:
```

# V-freq: 120.00 Hz  // h-freq: 124.14 KHz
Modeline "1280x960" 315.80  1280 1440 1824 2544   960  960  965 1034 -hsync +vsync

```

---

### Post by Wim Sturkenboom on 2007-05-23
Boot from live CD, mount harddisk partition, find xorg.conf and modify it back.

I suggest that you start with slightly more conservative settings (don't squeeze the last bit out (i.e. 1024x768@120Hz instead of at 150Hz.

### Post by tanelt on 2007-05-29
Just did a clean reinstall and also tried the modeline you suggested, powder. It gave me 1280x960@120Hz and it seemed to be  working just fine, but the viewable area of the screen was shrinked to the middle, and using the monitor's buttons to enlarge it was not enough to enlarge it to the whole screen. It just covered about 50%.


I think one can easily go insane before getting this thing to work. :frown:
